compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can configure import patterns for my project's tech stack
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add per-language import pattern configuration keys to dso-config.conf. Ship example patterns for 6 languages using ast-grep pattern syntax. Record-test-status.sh reads configured patterns to determine which ast-grep queries to run for centrality scoring.

**Why**: Different tech stacks have different import mechanisms (Python import, JS/TS import/require, Ruby require, Java import, Bash source). Configurable patterns make centrality scoring portable across projects.

**Scope**:
- IN: Config keys test_gate.import_pattern.<lang> in dso-config.conf, example patterns for Python, Flask, Ruby, Next.js/TypeScript, Java, Bash, record-test-status.sh reads patterns from config
- OUT: Pattern syntax validation at config-read time, auto-detection of project languages, runtime pattern correctness checks

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, .claude/dso-config.conf contains example import patterns for Python, Flask, Ruby, Next.js/TypeScript, Java, and Bash under test_gate.import_pattern.<lang> keys using ast-grep pattern syntax
  ← Satisfies: "Import patterns defined in config with examples for 6 languages"
- When this story is complete, record-test-status.sh reads import patterns from config rather than using any initial pattern mechanism
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] 6 import patterns must stay in sync with ast-grep pattern syntax; no runtime validation that patterns are syntactically correct
- [Testing] Patterns must be validated against real code structures for each language during development
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
